title: Early English Artistical Receipts (Wright's 1844 selection from Sloane MS 73)
summary: The checked-in XML is a repository representation and version of University of Michigan CME00136, which keys Thomas Wright's complete four-page article 'Early English Artistical Receipts' in the Archaeological Journal 1 (1844), pp. 152-155. Wright prints a modern introduction and twelve Middle English technical recipe/advice units from six discontinuous Sloane MS 73 loci, out of folio order: ff. 173v, 138v, 138r, 213r, 213v, and 215v. The article-year layer is 1844, while the completed bound carrier's title page is dated MDCCCXLV (1845); both are retained. A complete Internet Archive scan of that bound carrier and its OCR, metadata, page map, and scandata are cached with fixity. The current British Library record is a broad whole-composite description, leaves Digitised Content blank, and exposes no authorized public complete Sloane MS 73 image object as of 2026-07-13; onsite consultation and paid imaging remain available routes. MED indexes the selected text as (a1475) Recipe Painting(2), and MEG-C localizes only the target f. 138r-v hand to Bedfordshire. Clarke's 2018 concordance securely relates the target white-lead unit to Trinity Encyclopedia section 1 without establishing physical copying direction; the distinct complete 323-canvas Trinity digital facsimile is cached at a 1600-pixel request profile, and direct inspection locates that counterpart on provider page [1] p017 / IIIF canvas 21 only.
review_notes: The primary subject is the repository artifact at CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00136.xml. The U-M encoding, Wright article, completed bound carrier, Internet Archive scan, Sloane witness, catalog descriptions, and later scholarship are separate entities.
The cataloged historical unit is Wright's exact twelve-unit edited selection, not one continuous medieval codex section, all craft recipes in Sloane MS 73, or the reconstructed Trinity Encyclopedia.

The seven XML DIV2 elements represent one Wright introduction plus six manuscript-folio groups; fourteen medieval content paragraphs represent twelve recipe/advice units because two units split rubric from procedure.

The current British Library record's 0900-1799 range is for the entire composite and is not target-folio dating. Wright's earlier-fifteenth-century statement and MED's (a1475) stencil remain separately attributed.
MEG-C entry L4708 applies to the scribal text on ff. 95v-146. It can therefore contextualize target f. 138r-v only and is not generalized to ff. 173v, 213r-v, or 215v.

The current British Library record has an empty Digitised Content field and exact-shelfmark searches exposed no authorized complete public target-manuscript viewer or IIIF object as of 2026-07-13. This dated negative finding does not prove unpublished, internal, or legacy photography does not exist.

Trinity College MS O.9.39 is a public comparative physical witness, while Trinity's 323-canvas IIIF object is a separate digital facsimile with its own image access and rights. Clarke's concordance supports a textual-version relation for the white-lead portions only; direct image inspection maps that portion to provider page [1] p017 / canvas 21, and O.9.39 is not modeled as a physical ancestor of Sloane MS 73.
The pinned CME root LICENSE uses Unlicense language framed as software. Its application to XML/data/markup remains unresolved and is not broadened into a high-confidence rights claim.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00136
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00136; the file is 14,425 bytes.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-08-24 proofing and structure review, not the original encoding date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00136
entities.description: A keyed electronic transcription of Wright's complete four-page article, including the modern heading and introduction, six folio headnotes, twelve Middle English recipe/advice units, twelve editorial footnotes, the marginal vessel figure description, four print-page milestones, and signed closer.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, Early English Artistical Receipts (1844)
entities.description: The immediate source edition for CME00136: a four-page article under the recurring 'Original Documents' heading, with a modern introduction, folio labels, twelve selected Middle English recipe/advice units from Sloane MS 73, twelve explanatory footnotes, one reproduction of a marginal vessel drawing, and the signature T. WRIGHT.

date_statements.label: Article and journal-volume year
date_statements.notes: The separately modeled completed bound carrier has an 1845 title page.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, 'Original Documents, Illustrating the Arts, &c. of the Middle Ages: Early English Artistical Receipts,' The Archaeological Journal 1 (1844): 152-155.
bibliographic.title: Original Documents, Illustrating the Arts, &c. of the Middle Ages: Early English Artistical Receipts

notes: The printed order is f. 173v, f. 138v, f. 138r, f. 213r, f. 213v, f. 215v and therefore is neither continuous nor ascending manuscript folio order.
The article begins at the top of printed p. 152 and ends with T. WRIGHT. and footnotes at the foot of p. 155; p. 156 begins a separate proceedings section.

entities.label: The Archaeological Journal, volume I (journal year 1844; bound title page 1845)
entities.description: The completed bound serial carrier containing Wright's article on pp. 152-155. Internet Archive and journal bibliography identify volume 1 with 1844, while the scanned bound title page is explicitly dated MDCCCXLV (1845). These are separate date layers rather than one synthesized publication date.

date_statements.label: Journal volume designation and provider bibliographic year
date_statements.notes: This is the article and serial-volume year used by the journal citation and Internet Archive metadata.

date_statements.label: Completed bound-carrier title-page imprint
date_statements.notes: This is the date printed on the title page of the scanned bound volume, not a replacement for the article's 1844 citation year.

bibliographic.citation: The Archaeological Journal, vol. I (journal year 1844; completed bound title page: London, 1845).
bibliographic.title: The Archaeological Journal
bibliographic.publisher: Longman, Rrown, Green, and Longman [as printed on the bound title page]
bibliographic.date: 1845 bound title page; journal volume designated 1844

entities.label: Internet Archive complete scan of The Archaeological Journal, volume I
entities.description: A complete 482-page provider PDF of the bound volume, derived from 486 scanned images at 400 ppi. Wright's article is printed pp. 152-155, one-based PDF pages 170-173, and Internet Archive BookReader leaves n172-n175.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete-volume PDF with OCR text, provider metadata, page-number data, scandata, and browser page images
notes: Internet Archive leaf numbers and generated PDF page numbers diverge by two at the target: leaf n172 is one-based PDF page 170. Both systems were checked independently.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 73
entities.description: A composite multilingual manuscript miscellany of receipts, medicine, charms, and other material. Wright's twelve selected craft units are assigned to six discontinuous loci: ff. 138r-v, 173v, 213r-v, and 215v. The current British Library record does not isolate those target units, hands, or local dates and gives only the whole composite's broad 10th-18th-century range.

date_statements.label: Current catalog whole-composite date range
date_statements.notes: This is the current record's broad date range for the entire composite and must not be applied as target-folio dating.

date_statements.label: Wright's date statement for the manuscript containing the target receipts
date_statements.value: Earlier part of the fifteenth century
date_statements.notes: Wright supplies no codicological argument or hand-by-hand qualification.

date_statements.label: MED source-stencil date for Wright's selected text
date_statements.notes: This scholarly source date is retained separately from Wright's wording and the current whole-composite catalog range.

holding.notes: All manuscripts and archives are consulted at the British Library in London. Current physical requests require a valid Reader Pass and the exact shelfmark.

physical_description.extent: 1 item in the current British Library record
notes: The current record lists Dutch, English, French, and Latin, with Latin script, and gives no target-specific support, dimensions, collation, or folio total.
Otho Polwhele owned the manuscript in 1641; William Balstone and P- Balstone are also recorded as former owners.
The record's listed contents at ff. 4-7b, 10-25, 26, 28-50b, 89-95, and 98-146b are broad selected descriptions and do not delimit Wright's target craft units.

entities.label: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record 040-002112411
entities.description: The current official interim whole-manuscript record for Sloane MS 73, with identifiers, broad contents, whole-composite date range, languages, script, custodial history, physical-request route, and an empty digitised-content field. It does not isolate the target craft recipes or provide complete manuscript images.
notes: The page identifies the post-cyberattack catalog as an interim service; fields may be incomplete.

entities.label: Middle English Dictionary source stencil (a1475) Recipe Painting(2)
entities.description: The MED source designation used for quotations from Wright's Archaeological Journal 1 edition of Sloane 73, with target citations on pp. 152-155. The MED bibliography's separate preferred-manuscript entry for Sloane ff. 196-202 concerns another hand and is not a locator for Wright's target units.

entities.label: MEG-C Catalogue of Sources entry L4708 for Sloane MS 73
entities.description: A linguistic-corpus source entry defining one scribal text as the hand on Sloane MS 73 ff. 95v-146, with a corpus sample at ff. 100r-105v.2, Bedfordshire county assignment, date 15b1 (a1475, MED), and Cursiva anglicana media script. Of Wright's targets, only f. 138r-v falls within this hand range.

bibliographic.citation: Merja Stenroos and Kjetil V. Thengs, MEG-C Catalogue of Sources, version 2011.1 (University of Stavanger), entry L4708.
bibliographic.title: MEG-C Catalogue of Sources

notes: The catalogue introduction states that all entry information refers to the defined scribal text, not necessarily to the whole manuscript.

entities.label: Mark Clarke, Tricks of the Medieval Trades (2018)
entities.description: A modern translation and study of the Trinity Encyclopedia, a collection of seventy fourteenth-century English craft recipes, with Appendix I on Sloane MS 73. The official sample includes the T-S concordance and substantive variants needed here: Trinity section 1 corresponds to Sloane section C7, the target white-lead recipe on f. 213r, whose opening Clarke calls very compressed.

bibliographic.citation: Mark Clarke, Tricks of the Medieval Trades: The Trinity Encyclopedia, a Collection of Fourteenth-Century English Craft Recipes (London: Archetype Publications, 2018), Appendix I, especially pp. 94-95.
bibliographic.title: Tricks of the Medieval Trades: The Trinity Encyclopedia, a Collection of Fourteenth-Century English Craft Recipes

notes: Only the official six-page publisher sample was publicly accessible and cached. It is partial coverage of the complete book.
The complete Appendix concordance may establish additional target mappings, but they are not inferred from the sampled rows alone.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, MS O.9.39
entities.description: A five-part composite manuscript. Part I, dated by the current catalog to the fourteenth or fifteenth century, is an imperfect Book of Receipts in Middle English, mostly for colors and dyes. Clarke designates it MS T and relates its section 1 textually to Sloane MS S section C7. It is a comparative witness, not a demonstrated physical exemplar for Sloane MS 73.

holding.notes: Catalog access to the physical witness is recorded here. The Universal Viewer and IIIF image object are modeled separately as facsimile:trinity:O-9-39-complete-2024.

physical_description.extent: Five volumes bound together: I. 36 ff.; II. 12 ff.; III. 123 pp.; IV. 20 ff.; V. 40 ff.
notes: Part I is vellum, 49 lines per page, and imperfect at the beginning.
The catalog lists Italian, Latin, and Middle English for the composite.

entities.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge digital facsimile of MS O.9.39
entities.description: Trinity College Cambridge's complete published 323-canvas digital object for the five-part composite MS O.9.39. The sequence contains 320 ordinary-light object canvases from the front cover through the back cover, followed by three ultraviolet canvases for Part I provider pages p001, p016, and p033.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with 323 color canvases and IIIF Image API 2 services; 320 ordinary-light object canvases plus three ultraviolet canvases
notes: Completeness is relative to all 323 canvases in the provider's published manifest, not an inference that every physically possible exterior or analytical image exists.
The three ultraviolet canvases repeat provider pages [1] p001, p016, and p033 in another imaging mode and remain part of the complete provider object.
Direct inspection securely maps Clarke's MS T section 1 only to provider page [1] p017 / IIIF canvas 21; no other canvas is assigned to that textual counterpart.

notes: The article closes with the printed signature T. WRIGHT.

notes: The volume title page says the journal was published under the committee's direction.

scope.notes: The repository artifact and U-M item are materially the same keyed text, but the checked evidence does not establish a directional technical transfer process.

object.description: The complete four-page printed article, including modern apparatus and twelve medieval units.

subject.description: The twelve Middle English recipe/advice units, excluding Wright's modern introduction, folio headnotes, footnotes, and signature.

object.description: Six discontinuous manuscript loci in Wright's printed sequence.

scope.notes: Wright explicitly says the receipts are taken from Sloane MS 73. Exact item boundaries, neighboring texts, hands, and editorial interventions require manuscript collation.

subject.description: The four-page article within the completed bound volume.

scope.notes: The article/year 1844 and bound-title-page year 1845 are retained separately.

subject.description: The complete 482-page provider PDF and associated derivatives.

object.description: The complete bound volume, including boards and preliminary matter represented in the scan.
Sloane MS 73 as one composite item.

scope.notes: The record's broad fields do not isolate Wright's selected target units.

subject.description: The MED source stencil and associated target quotations.

object.description: The Middle English recipe text, not Wright's modern apparatus.

scope.notes: MED indexes the source as (a1475) Recipe Painting(2) in Archaeol.J.1 (Sln 73).

object.description: The hand on ff. 95v-146, which includes target f. 138r-v only.

scope.notes: The Bedfordshire and 15b1/a1475 data are hand-specific and are not applied to the other target loci.

subject.description: Appendix I concordance and substantial-variants discussion.

object.description: The target white-lead unit printed by Wright from f. 213r.

scope.notes: Clarke identifies the Sloane opening as very compressed relative to the corresponding Trinity section.

subject.description: The translated Trinity Encyclopedia and Appendix I concordance.

object.description: Part I Book of Receipts, specifically the section Clarke numbers T section 1. Direct image inspection finds the rubric 'For to make white leed' and the complete section before the next rubric on one provider canvas.

scope.notes: The current Trinity catalog identifies Part I generally; Clarke supplies the T section numbering and textual concordance.

object.description: The corresponding Trinity Encyclopedia white-lead section only, beginning with the rubric 'For to make white leed' and ending before the next rubric on the same provider canvas.

scope.notes: Version_of is a qualified textual relation between recipe portions. It does not assert that either surviving physical manuscript was copied directly from the other, and it is not generalized to Wright's other eleven units.

subject.description: The complete published 323-canvas IIIF object, including 320 ordinary-light canvases and the three ultraviolet canvases appended by the provider.

object.description: The five-part composite physical MS O.9.39 represented from front cover through back cover in the provider sequence.

scope.notes: Whole-object scope is relative to the provider's complete published digital inventory. The 1600-pixel research bundle is an access derivative, not a claim to contain preservation-master image resolution.

access.access_method: Exact catalog record, exact-shelfmark catalog search, current digitised-manuscripts guidance, and public web/repository searches checked for a complete authorized object
access.format: No complete public target-manuscript image resource established
notes: The exact record's Digitised Content field is empty and exposes no thumbnail, viewer, or IIIF link. Exact-shelfmark searches found no authorized complete public Sloane MS 73 facsimile.
This is a dated negative access finding, not proof that unpublished, internal, selected, legacy, or privately held photography does not exist. No restricted or selected manuscript images were cached.

access.access_method: Request the exact shelfmark with a valid Reader Pass; consult manuscripts and archives in London
notes: Readers who registered or renewed after 21 March 2024 may request in advance; older passes require renewal. Availability and handling restrictions remain subject to staff review.

access.access_method: British Library Digitisation Services order; quotation, preservation assessment, and exact reuse permission required
access.contact: British Library Digitisation Services; permissions@bl.uk for reuse permission
access.format: Manuscript photography in approved delivery formats
notes: For complete-object coverage, request covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, every unfoliated flyleaf and blank, both sides of every folio in the current object, edges where offered, final pastedown, and detached matter. Ask staff to confirm the full current foliation and image inventory because the interim catalog gives only '1 item'.
Map the work separately to ff. 138r-v, 173v, 213r-v, and 215v. Target-leaf crops alone are partial work-focused coverage, not a complete manuscript facsimile.
The service states that original manuscripts cannot be scanned; precious or rare items require photography. Reproduction and republication require the exact supplied terms and permissions.

access.access_method: Middle English Compendium bibliography and dictionary source citations
notes: Current public search indexing exposes the record and target quotations. Direct automated requests intermittently encounter a Cloudflare challenge.

access.access_method: University of Stavanger public PDF download

notes: The PDF passed qpdf syntax and stream checks. The host required ordinary browser request headers for download; the file itself is publicly linked and unrestricted.

locators: MS T section 1 = MS S section C7
notes: The cached file is the complete six-page publisher sample but partial coverage of the 132-page book.
qpdf reported two non-fatal hint-table/shared-identifier warnings while successfully parsing the PDF; text extraction and page rendering succeeded.
Public access to the sample does not imply an open license for the book or sample.

access.access_method: Trinity College Cambridge James Catalogue record
Complete Universal Viewer and IIIF Presentation manifest

local_copies.label: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF manifest for O.9.39

work_portion.label: Comparative counterpart to the target Sloane f. 213r white-lead unit
locators: MS T section 1 corresponds textually to Sloane MS S section C7
notes: The cached manifest enumerates all 323 provider canvases but contains no image binaries; the separately listed IIIF ZIP retains every canvas image.
Direct inspection securely locates section 1 on provider page [1] p017 / canvas 21, beginning with the rubric 'For to make white leed' and ending before the next rubric on the same canvas.
No physical-source ancestry from O.9.39 to Sloane MS 73 is asserted.

local_copies.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge MS O.9.39 IIIF facsimile bundle

work_portion.label: Comparative counterpart to the target Sloane f. 213r white-lead unit
locators: MS T section 1 corresponds textually to Sloane MS S section C7
notes: Direct inspection of p017 and its adjacent canvases securely bounds T section 1 to p017 alone: it begins with the rubric 'For to make white leed' and ends before the following rubric on the same canvas.
The p016 and p018 images were inspected only to confirm the boundary; they are not included in the mapped textual counterpart.
No physical-source ancestry from O.9.39 to Sloane MS 73 is asserted.

Complete provider inventory of 323 canvases: canvases 1-320 form the ordinary-light object sequence from front cover through back cover, and canvases 321-323 are the provider's ultraviolet repetitions of [1] p001, p016, and p033.

The requested maximum-width profile was IIIF size segment 1600,. It was used for 315 canvases; eight narrow Part IV stub canvases (219-220, 223-224, 231-232, and 235-236) were retained at full native width, 772 or 774 pixels, instead of being upscaled.
ZIP contains the exact provider manifest, a deterministic exact-source inventory with per-image byte counts and SHA-256 values, and all 323 JPEG derivatives.

access.format: 323-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object and Image API 2 JPEG services; 320 ordinary-light canvases plus three ultraviolet canvases
notes: The catalog reports that currently displayed images were recaptured at higher resolution in October 2024.
The ignored local ZIP is a private research cache under the provider's CC BY-NC 4.0 license; public availability and redistribution are separate questions.

rights.component: Underlying fifteenth-century Middle English verbal material represented by the twelve target units
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions where historical copyright terms have expired
rights.basis: The underlying verbal material is attested in a fifteenth-century manuscript and was published in 1844; no modern intellectual contribution is included in this component.

rights.component: Wright's 1844 editorial selection, introduction, notes, figure reproduction, and typography
rights.basis: The article was published in London in 1844 and is in the public domain in the United States by age.
Internet Archive metadata marks the item possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT; this is a provider status rather than an affirmative open-content license.

rights.component: University of Michigan keyed electronic text and presentation
rights.basis: The CME collection identifies its public-domain source editions and presents No Copyright-United States framing; this is a provider rights statement, not an open license for every modern layer.

rights.component: Modern XML transcription, structure, and metadata
rights.basis: The pinned repository root LICENSE contains Unlicense text framed as software, but no path-specific statement establishes that it intentionally covers corpus XML/data/markup.
notes: Do not treat this caution about the modern XML layer as restricting the underlying medieval recipes or 1844 edition.

rights.basis: The catalog page states that all text is available under CC BY except where otherwise stated.
notes: This license for catalog text does not license the physical manuscript, future manuscript photography, or third-party content.

rights.component: New British Library manuscript photography and supplied digital files
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and contractual delivery context
rights.basis: Imaging Services requires an order, preservation assessment, payment, and written permission for copying or reproduction. No order or manuscript-specific license has been obtained.

rights.component: MEG-C Catalogue of Sources text and PDF
rights.basis: The modern institutional PDF is publicly downloadable, but no open license was exposed in the checked file or download context.

rights.component: Clarke's 2018 book and official publisher sample
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and international copyright context
rights.basis: The modern publication is offered commercially and the publisher site states all rights reserved; the public sample carries no open license.
notes: The cached sample is retained as research evidence and is not represented as licensed for repository redistribution.

rights.component: The complete 323-canvas Trinity College photographic facsimile of MS O.9.39, including the three ultraviolet canvases
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest declares CC BY-NC 4.0, and the manuscript catalog page states that Trinity's work is copyright the Master and Fellows of Trinity College, Cambridge and licensed under the same terms.
notes: This record is scoped to the photographic digital facsimile, not the physical witness or the underlying medieval text.
The catalog record remains a separate witness-access route and is not conflated with the licensed image object.
The complete local bundle is Git-ignored research evidence; redistribution must comply with CC BY-NC 4.0 and corpus publication choices remain separate from cache availability.

editorial_practices.description: Wright selected twelve craft recipe/advice units from six discontinuous Sloane MS 73 loci and printed them in a sequence that is not manuscript folio order.
editorial_practices.scope: The complete four-page article, especially the Middle English text on pp. 152-155
editorial_practices.source_feature: Craft material dispersed across ff. 138r-v, 173v, 213r-v, and 215v within a much larger composite manuscript
editorial_practices.representation: Six folio-labeled groups ordered f. 173v, 138v, 138r, 213r, 213v, 215v, with twelve units and modern apparatus
notes: Selection is confirmed; normalization, omissions, corrections, and the use of any intermediary transcript remain unresolved.

editorial_practices.description: The U-M item selects and keys the complete Wright article from the larger journal volume, preserving its modern introduction, folio labels, medieval text, notes, figure description, page breaks, and signature.
editorial_practices.representation: One DIV1 with one introduction DIV2 and six document DIV2s; four PB milestones; twelve NOTE elements; one FIGURE description
notes: Complete here means complete relative to the four-page article, not the journal volume or physical manuscript.

editorial_practices.description: Clarke supplies a T-S concordance and translates substantial Sloane variants against the Trinity Encyclopedia text.
editorial_practices.scope: Appendix I; current target evidence at p. 94 for MS T section 1 = MS S section C7
editorial_practices.source_feature: Textually corresponding but variant craft-recipe passages in Trinity College MS O.9.39 and British Library Sloane MS 73
editorial_practices.representation: Section concordance plus selected Sloane variant translations, identifying the Sloane white-lead opening as very compressed
notes: The collation supports textual relationship, not direct physical-manuscript ancestry.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00136.xml.
evidence.summary: Identifies CME00136 and preserves the complete encoded article: headings, modern introduction, six folio groups, fourteen medieval content paragraphs representing twelve recipe/advice units, twelve notes, one figure description, four page breaks, and signed closer T. WRIGHT.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural and textual audit of CME00136.xml.
evidence.locator: ETS/TEXT element counts and complete paragraph sequence
evidence.summary: Counts 1 DIV1, 7 DIV2, 2 HEAD, 6 HEADNOTE, 21 P, 12 NOTE, 1 FIGURE, and 4 PB elements. The P total comprises one Wright introduction, six folio-headnote paragraphs, and fourteen medieval content paragraphs; rubric-plus-procedure splits reduce the intellectual count to twelve recipe/advice units.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, 'Original Documents, Illustrating the Arts, &c. of the Middle Ages: Early English Artistical Receipts,' The Archaeological Journal 1 (1844): 152-155.
evidence.locator: Printed pp. 152-155; PDF pages 170-173; IA leaves n172-n175
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for the complete printed selection, heading, introduction, direct Sloane claim, six folio labels and order, twelve unit boundaries and readings, editorial notes and vessel figure, article signature, page boundary, and article/year 1844.

evidence.citation: The Archaeological Journal, volume I, completed bound title page in Internet Archive item archaeologicaljo01brit.
evidence.locator: One-based PDF page 7; title-page imprint ending MDCCCXLV
evidence.summary: Direct visual evidence that the completed bound volume title page is dated MDCCCXLV (1845) and identifies volume I, London, and the British Archaeological Association Central Committee. This carrier date is recorded separately from the article and journal-volume year 1844.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for archaeologicaljo01brit, The Archaeological Journal.
evidence.locator: metadata object and PDF/OCR/page-data file records
evidence.summary: Supports item identity, provider date 1844, Getty Research Institute contribution, Sloan Foundation sponsorship, 400-ppi scanning, complete PDF and OCR filenames and sizes, provider checksums, public access, and possible-copyright-status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT.
notes: The live endpoint includes dynamic fields; the cached response is the exact object described by this checksum.
Provider date 1844 is not used to erase the visually confirmed 1845 bound title-page imprint.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive page-number and scandata derivatives plus direct PDF page-sequence inspection for archaeologicaljo01brit.
evidence.locator: pageNumber 152-155 at leafNum 172-175; visual PDF pages 170-173
evidence.summary: Page data maps printed pp. 152-155 to BookReader leaves n172-n175. Direct PDF inspection maps the same four pages to one-based PDF pages 170-173 and shows that PDF page 174 begins a separate proceedings section.
notes: The companion cached scandata file is 253,621 bytes with SHA-256 3e7d71273cd4ef489ab9ea62b676ed70db4482ef1cef216ff462c0ff2b38fa88.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00136.
evidence.summary: Supplies the public item identity and stable CME00136 route; the collection browse labels the anonymous group 'Artists' recipes' and cites Wright's Early English artistical receipts in Archaeological Journal 1 (1844). Automated item retrieval encountered a challenge, so exact text was checked in the pinned repository XML and source scan.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
evidence.summary: Describes manual keying from public-domain editions, in-house proofreading and editing, public educational and research access, bulk XML, and the collection's No Copyright-United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Contains Unlicense wording framed as software; it does not expressly scope the license to XML/data/markup, so application to the modern corpus layer remains uncertain.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Sloane MS 73, record 040-002112411.
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, record ID, MDARK, whole-composite 0900-1799 range, broad contents, Dutch/English/French/Latin languages, Latin script, extent one item, custodial history, physical request and reference-service routes, blank Digitised Content field, and CC BY licensing of catalog text.
notes: The current page is an interim catalog after the British Library cyberattack and may not reproduce all legacy descriptive detail.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts exact-shelfmark search and current digitised-manuscripts guidance for Sloane MS 73.
evidence.locator: Exact Sloane MS 73 record; Digitised Content field and public viewer routes
evidence.summary: The exact current record leaves Digitised Content blank and exposes no thumbnail, viewer, or IIIF link. Searches of the current catalog, legacy viewer terms, exact shelfmark, and public repositories found no authorized complete public Sloane MS 73 facsimile as of the access date.
notes: This is a dated negative finding, not proof that unpublished, internal, selected, or legacy photography does not exist.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation Services.
evidence.summary: Supplies the current paid order route and required shelfmark, title, page or folio sides, format, payment, and delivery details; states that original manuscripts cannot be scanned and rare items may require expert photography.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms, updated January 2026.
evidence.summary: Supports single-page imaging conditions, preservation-suitability review, fees, delivery restrictions, third-party copyright caution, and the requirement for written permission before copying or reproducing supplied material.

evidence.citation: Middle English Dictionary, bibliography entry 'Recipes from BL Sloane 73' and target dictionary quotations under source stencil (a1475) Recipe Painting(2) in Archaeol.J.1 (Sln 73).
evidence.locator: BIB5553 cross-note; target quotations from Archaeological Journal pp. 152-155
evidence.summary: Supports the source-stencil title and date (a1475) Recipe Painting(2), journal and Sloane identifiers, and exact target quotation locators. BIB5553 separately describes a preferred manuscript source at Sloane ff. 196-202 and an Essex hand; that separate locus is not transferred to Wright's target folios.
notes: Direct automated access intermittently encountered a Cloudflare challenge; current official search indexing and multiple dictionary pages exposed the same source-stencil data and quotations.

evidence.citation: Merja Stenroos and Kjetil V. Thengs, MEG-C Catalogue of Sources, version 2011.1 (University of Stavanger), entry L4708.
evidence.summary: Defines the Sloane 73 scribal text as the hand on ff. 95v-146, sample ff. 100r-105v.2, and assigns Bedfordshire, date 15b1 (a1475, MED), genre Alchemica, and Cursiva anglicana media. The catalogue introduction states that entry data apply only to the defined scribal text; only target f. 138r-v falls within it.
notes: The complete 227-page PDF is 1,150,249 bytes and passed qpdf checks.

evidence.citation: Archetype Publications, Tricks of the Medieval Trades: The Trinity Encyclopedia, a Collection of Fourteenth-Century English Craft Recipes, by Mark Clarke.
evidence.summary: Supports author, full title, anonymous fourteenth-century English craft-recipe subject, seventy-recipe scope, contents including Appendix I on Sloane 73, ISBN 9781909492653, 132 pages, October 2018 publication, commercial availability, and official sample route.

evidence.citation: Mark Clarke, Tricks of the Medieval Trades (Archetype Publications, 2018), official sample, Appendix I, pp. 94-95.
evidence.locator: Book p. 94 / sample PDF p. 5, Concordance T-S and MS T section 1 = MS S section C7
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact concordance between Trinity section 1 and Sloane section C7, translates the opening of the target white-lead recipe, and calls the Sloane beginning very compressed. It supports a target-specific textual-version relation but no copying direction between physical manuscripts.
notes: The cached official sample is 152,717 bytes and contains six pages; it is partial coverage of the complete book.
qpdf parsed the file with non-fatal hint-table/shared-identifier warnings; text extraction and direct page inspection succeeded.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, O.9.39 (Five Medical Texts / Medica).
evidence.locator: Main Details, Contents I, Notes, IIIF Manifest URL, and rights footer
evidence.summary: Supports shelfmark, titles, James number, composite dates and physical description, Part I's imperfect English Book of Receipts mostly for colors and dyes, and Middle English language. It separately advertises the Universal Viewer and IIIF manifest, states that the current images were recaptured at higher resolution in October 2024, and applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to Trinity's work.
notes: This record describes the physical witness and its catalog route; the linked image object is represented by separate facsimile and digital-surrogate records.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, IIIF Presentation 2 manifest for MS O.9.39.
evidence.locator: Complete 323-canvas sequence; canvases 1-320 ordinary-light object sequence; canvases 321-323 UV repetitions of [1] p001, p016, and p033
evidence.summary: The exact provider manifest identifies O.9.39, attributes the digital object to Trinity College Cambridge, declares CC BY-NC 4.0, enumerates 323 canvases with IIIF Image API 2 services, runs from the front cover through the back cover in canvases 1-320, and appends three ultraviolet canvases for Part I pages p001, p016, and p033.
notes: The 258,496-byte cached manifest is metadata-only; the separate IIIF ZIP contains the same manifest, a deterministic inventory, and all 323 image derivatives.

evidence.citation: Complete locally cached Trinity College Cambridge MS O.9.39 IIIF facsimile bundle, generated from the official Presentation 2 manifest.
evidence.summary: The audited bundle preserves all 323 provider canvases in manifest order, including the three terminal UV canvases, with the exact official manifest, deterministic inventory, successful image URL for each canvas, byte count, and per-image SHA-256. It is complete relative to the provider's published inventory.
notes: The ZIP is 1,145,743,554 bytes and contains 325 members: manifest.json, inventory.json, and 323 JPEG images.
All 323 inventory items are unique and present. Canvases 1-320 are the ordinary-light object sequence and canvases 321-323 are UV repetitions of [1] p001, p016, and p033.

The 1600, request profile was used for 315 canvases. Eight Part IV stub canvases narrower than 1600 pixels in the provider manifest were retained with the full native-width request rather than upscaled.

evidence.citation: Direct visual inspection of Trinity College Cambridge MS O.9.39, Part I, provider page p017, with adjacent p016 and p018 boundary checks.
evidence.locator: inventory item 21 / images/000021.jpg; provider part [1], p017; IIIF canvas 21, label 1-p017; adjacent boundary checks at items 20 and 22
evidence.summary: Canvas 21 opens with the rubric 'For to make white leed' and contains the complete recipe that Clarke numbers MS T section 1, ending before the following rubric on that same canvas. Combined with Clarke's concordance, this securely maps only T section 1 on p017 to Sloane section C7; it does not establish copying ancestry.
notes: The exact cached p017 member is 3,163,567 bytes with SHA-256 504a4d90a44bfa30a4e2d3e897244eb7ffbc213b208aea1de78b1374a0cd17f5.
The direct image request is the recorded 1600, IIIF derivative; the provider returned a 1601 x 2371 JPEG.
Adjacent p016 and p018 were inspected to verify the section boundary only and are not assigned to the counterpart.

open_questions.question: Can the British Library supply or authorize a complete color digital facsimile of the entire current physical Sloane MS 73, and what exact storage, Git-publication, viewer, print-on-demand, and commercial permissions would govern it?
next_steps: Request the complete current physical object, not only target leaves, and require a supplied image inventory tied to current foliation.
Map the work separately to ff. 138r-v, 173v, 213r-v, and 215v, with adjacent surfaces for boundary analysis.
Retain quotation, correspondence, delivered checksums, and exact manuscript-specific license; do not describe an order as pending or permission as granted until an actual request receives a response.

open_questions.question: What exact manuscript boundaries, neighboring texts, hands, sequence positions, and local dates correspond to all twelve target units?
next_steps: Inspect complete target and adjacent manuscript surfaces and make a line-by-line concordance with Wright.
Apply MEG-C L4708 only to f. 138r-v unless palaeographic evidence extends that hand.
Keep Wright's earlier-fifteenth-century date, MED's a1475 stencil, and the current broad whole-composite range separately attributed.

open_questions.question: What selection, normalization, expansion, punctuation, correction, omission, or reordering did Wright apply to the manuscript readings?
next_steps: Collate every printed unit, rubric, folio label, note, and the jordan figure against manuscript images.
Investigate the printed 'etc.' in the fine-azure recipe without assigning a cause in advance.
Determine whether Wright used the codex directly or an intermediary transcript.

open_questions.question: Which remaining target units have secure counterparts in the Trinity Encyclopedia or other craft-recipe traditions, and what relationship can be demonstrated?
next_steps: Consult Clarke's complete 2018 Appendix I and 2016 edition for target-specific concordances and variants.
Retain the current white-lead C7/T1 relationship without extrapolating it to other target recipes.
Separate abstract textual versions and reconstructed collections from physical witnesses and direct-copy claims.

open_questions.question: What exact issue date and wrapper history explains an 1844 article and journal volume inside a completed bound carrier dated 1845?
next_steps: Consult original issue wrappers, publisher records, and authoritative serial bibliography.
Preserve article and journal-volume year 1844 separately from completed bound title-page year 1845.
Do not synthesize the two layers into one unqualified publication date.
